ops.title: Ship each work stream the moment its code lands and lane-level gates pass — deep verification trails behind, fixes go forward as follow-up commits
ops.body:
During the decision-board program (2026-07-19) the user directed: "ship the ios and web as soon as they are done, then you can do verify and whatnot after … same for CLI, etc" — after earlier pushing "start parallelizing things more, you're going too slow."

**Why:** the user values throughput and continuous integration over gated perfection; long-running deep verifies (behavioral browser E2E, adversarial reviews) should not hold completed code hostage. The repo's push-dance already tolerates this (path-scoped jj/`ccx vcs ship` commits from a shared working copy, fix-forward on red CI).

**How to apply:** ship each stream path-scoped via `ccx vcs ship` as soon as its implementation lanes finish and their own gates (typecheck/unit/sim build) are green. Let heavyweight verification (E2E, refuters) run after or in parallel; land its findings as follow-up commits. Don't batch multiple finished streams waiting for a joint verdict. Related: [[cc-present-push-dance]].
